ec2.trafficmirrorfilterrule.CfnAbout:
Creates a Traffic Mirror filter rule.

A Traffic Mirror rule defines the Traffic Mirror source traffic to mirror.

You need the Traffic Mirror filter ID when you create the rule.

ec2.trafficmirrorfilterrule.Description.Description: The description of the Traffic Mirror rule.

ec2.trafficmirrorfilterrule.DestinationC.Description: The destination CIDR block to assign to the Traffic Mirror rule.

ec2.trafficmirrorfilterrule.Protocol.Description: The protocol, for example UDP, to assign to the Traffic Mirror rule.  For information about the protocol value, see [Protocol Numbers](https://www.iana.org/assignments/protocol-numbers/protocol-numbers.xhtml) on the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA) website.

ec2.trafficmirrorfilterrule.RuleAction.Description: The action to take on the filtered traffic.

ec2.trafficmirrorfilterrule.RuleNumber.Description: The number of the Traffic Mirror rule. This number must be unique for each Traffic Mirror rule in a given direction. The rules are processed in ascending order by rule number.

ec2.trafficmirrorfilterrule.SourceCidrBl.Description: The source CIDR block to assign to the Traffic Mirror rule.

ec2.trafficmirrorfilterrule.TrafficMirro.Description: The ID of the filter that this rule is associated with.
